guide.title: Manage people and organization access separately
guide.summary: Creating a person is not the same as granting access to an organization.
body: Create or sync the person first, then grant organization access through memberships.
This keeps identity administration separate from operational access governance.

concepts.label: Local Person
concepts.summary: A locally managed person record with username, email, and optional login methods.
concepts.why_it_exists: The platform needs a built-in way to create administrators, local users, temporary users, or external collaborators even when no external directory is available.
how_to_use: Create local people in administration when they need a platform identity.
Grant organization access separately through memberships.

relations.summary: A local person is represented by a principal for access checks.
